title: ServiceRecord
description: A structured service encounter record designed to capture the data elements required by major federal DV/SA grant programs (VAWA, VOCA, FVPSA). This schema enables advocates to document service delivery in a way that satisfies grant reporting requirements while maintaining survivor privacy. No PII is stored — only demographic categories and service types needed for aggregate reporting.

recordId.description: Unique identifier for this service record. Use a sequential number, UUID, or internal case number. Do not use the survivor's name or other PII.

clientId.description: Internal client identifier used to track services across encounters without using PII. Same individual across multiple records should use the same clientId.

serviceDate.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) on which the service was provided.

serviceType.description: Type(s) of service provided during this encounter. Categories align with VAWA/VOCA/FVPSA reporting requirements.

minutes.description: Length of the encounter in minutes.

shelterNights.description: Number of shelter bed-nights provided (for shelter services only).

victimizationType.description: Type(s) of victimization experienced by the client. Used for aggregate reporting — not for clinical assessment.

relationshipToOffender.description: Relationship between the survivor and the person who caused harm. Required by most grant reports.

demographics.description: Demographic information collected for aggregate grant reporting ONLY. These fields should NEVER be used to identify an individual. Collection is voluntary — if the survivor declines to provide any field, use 'not-disclosed'. These categories align with federal grant reporting requirements.

ageRange.description: Age range, not exact age — to protect privacy while meeting reporting requirements.

race.description: Race category(ies). Select all that apply. Categories match federal reporting standards.

disability.description: Whether the client has a disability that affects service needs.

primaryLanguage.description: Primary language spoken by the client. Relevant for language access reporting.

immigrationStatus.description: Immigration-related status, if relevant to services provided. Only collect with consent and only if it affects service delivery.

lgbtq.description: Whether the client identifies as LGBTQ+. Some grants require this for reporting.

householdSize.description: Number of people in the client's household, including children.

dependentChildren.description: Number of dependent children in the client's care.

programFunding.description: Which grant program(s) fund the service being documented. A single service encounter may be reported under multiple grants.

grantSubCategory.description: Specific grant sub-program if applicable — e.g., 'VAWA STOP', 'VAWA Legal Assistance for Victims', 'VOCA Victim Assistance', 'FVPSA Shelter', 'FVPSA Prevention'.

outcome.description: Service outcome information. Used for grant performance measurement and program improvement.

safetyPlanCreatedOrUpdated.description: Whether a safety plan was created or updated during this encounter.

protectiveOrderObtained.description: Whether a protective order was obtained as a result of advocacy during this encounter.

shelterPlaced.description: Whether the client was placed in emergency or transitional shelter.

housingSecured.description: Whether permanent or transitional housing was secured.

legalRepresentationSecured.description: Whether the client was connected with legal representation.

referralsMade.description: Agencies or service types the client was referred to.

referralsCompleted.description: Referrals where the client successfully connected with the receiving agency (warm handoff completed).

clientSafetyImproved.description: Advocate's assessment of whether the client's safety situation improved as a result of this service.

notes.description: Any additional outcome notes relevant to program reporting.

staffing.description: Staffing information for the service encounter. Used for program management and grant FTE reporting.

staffRole.description: Role of the person who provided the service.

staffId.description: Internal staff identifier. Do not use names in records that may be shared.

interpreterUsed.description: Whether a language interpreter was used during the service.

interpreterLanguage.description: Language pair for the interpreter, if used.

reportingPeriod.description: The grant reporting period this service falls within. Helps with batch reporting.

reportingDeadline.description: Date by which this data must be submitted to the funder.

notes.description: Any additional notes about this service encounter. Do not include PII — this field is for program notes, not case notes.
